description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a military-style olive green fuselage and dual wings, each adorned with roundel insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century British aircraft. The airplane features a wooden two-blade propeller at the nose, supported by struts connecting the upper and lower wings. The tail section is marked by a vertical stabilizer with red, white, and blue striping, and a horizontal stabilizer with squared tips. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible pilot seat and control stick. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable.
description: A vintage olive green biplane with roundel insignias, wooden propeller, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the dual wings, roundel insignias, wooden propeller, and tail with tricolor vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the squared tail surfaces, tricolor vertical stabilizer, and open cockpit behind the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the roundel insignias and propeller.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, engine cowling, and the biplane's stacked wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; squared tips and tricolor rudder.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a fixed tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, with no canopy.
